bug_fix_report.reproduction: Replace the fixed 200 ms sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable export completion event; this deterministically reproduces the missing wake-up whenever the worker registers after the queue transition.
bug_fix_report.root_cause: The worker listener can register after the one-shot completion transition, causing the wake-up to be lost.
confirming_evidence: All captured failures checked 8–47 ms before worker completion, while passing runs happened to complete within 200 ms.
With listener registration ordered before the transition, the stabilized reproduction passed 500/500 runs.
When only the ordering fix was removed, the stabilized test failed within 12 runs with the same missing wake-up.
bug_fix_report.fix_boundary: Register the listener before the transition; leave other production behavior unchanged, retain the condition-based test wait, and remove the fixed sleep.
bug_fix_report.regression_protection: Keep the condition-based completion-event test and hand it to pc-tdd; the stabilized check passed 500 consecutive runs with the fix.

debug_journal.hypothesis: Failing runs check for export completion before the worker completes.
debug_journal.prediction: Failure timestamps will show the check occurring before worker completion, while passing runs will complete within 200 ms.
debug_journal.experiment: Instrument event enqueue and worker completion timestamps without changing the wait.
debug_journal.result: Every captured failure checked 8–47 ms before completion; passing runs happened to complete within 200 ms.

debug_journal.hypothesis: A condition-based wait will remove timing luck and reveal whether the completion event is eventually emitted.
debug_journal.prediction: The race will become deterministic and expose any missing wake-up instead of becoming green through reruns.
debug_journal.experiment: Replace only the fixed sleep with a condition-based wait on the observable completion event, leaving production code unchanged.
debug_journal.result: The test deterministically reproduced a missing wake-up whenever the worker registered after the queue transition.

debug_journal.hypothesis: Registering the listener after the one-shot transition loses the wake-up.
debug_journal.prediction: Registering the listener before the transition will make the stabilized reproduction pass consistently, and removing only that ordering change will restore the same failure.
debug_journal.experiment: Change only the worker registration ordering so the listener exists before the transition.
debug_journal.result: The stabilized reproduction passed 500/500 runs; removing only the ordering fix restored the same missing wake-up within 12 runs.
